stat_of_week: 19 HR - BaseballPro77 set a new single-week team home run record, with Trout (5), Murakami (4), and Riley (3) combining for 12 of the 19 in a dominant 7-2 demolition of the Reapers
thriller_note: A dead-even tie where neither team could separate in the pitching or hitting categories left both teams splitting points when each desperately needed a full win, keeping both squads mired in the middle of the standings heading into a pivotal Week 5.
key_storyline: BaseballPro77 put on the most dominant offensive performance of the season, setting a new league record with 19 home runs and 39 RBI while posting a 2.33 ERA to crush the Reapers 7-2. Trout, Murakami, and Riley each had monster weeks, and the pitching staff — led by Williams and Kirby — matched the offense blow for blow, signaling that this team is rounding into form at exactly the right time. With a showdown against Abbotsford-Colby Twins on the horizon, BaseballPro77 looks like the most dangerous team in the league right now.
lucky_reason: Despite posting just 4 HR, a 4.40 ERA, and only 2 quality starts — among the weakest stat lines of any team this week — the Benchwarmers somehow carry the highest positive luck delta in the league at +1.21, meaning their actual wins are dramatically outpacing what their production deserves.
unlucky_reason: The Twins won their matchup 6-4 but carry the worst luck delta in the league at -0.61, meaning their strong category production — including 14 HR, 8 SB, and a dominant José Ramírez week — is consistently generating far fewer wins than their output should produce.
records_broken: BaseballPro77 set two new single-week records: most HR by a team with 19 (previous mark: unknown prior week) and most RBI by a team with 39, matching the previous record also set this week — both are new league highs for Week 4.
power_rankings: 1. BaseballPro77 | Record-setting 19 HR and 39 RBI with a 2.33 ERA — this is the most complete performance of the season and the hottest team in the league right now | up
2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Lost the matchup but still put up 72 K and 4 QS; Ryan Weathers (18 K, 1.05 WHIP) is an ace-level performer and this offense remains elite over four weeks | same

3. General Hospital | An 8-2 blowout win driven by 6 QS, a 2.68 ERA, a 0.96 WHIP, and Will Warren (17 K, 1.69 ERA) — this pitching staff is the best in the league and Kumar Rocker adds even more depth | up
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge (5 HR, 7 RBI), Bailey Ober (17 K), and a 3.01 ERA with 5 QS in a clean 5-2 win shows the rotation is finally healthy enough to complement the elite offense | up

5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Eight quality starts is a league record-threatening number; Paul Skenes (0.90 ERA, 0.50 WHIP), Imanaga, and Valdez make this the deepest rotation in the game, even if the offense remains quiet | up

6. Albuquerque Bombers | Byron Buxton (3 HR, 8 R) and Ivan Herrera (2 HR) fueled a 5-4 win; aggressive waiver activity keeps plugging holes, and a Week 5 matchup with the struggling Reapers is a golden opportunity | same

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez (4 HR, 5 SB, .533 OBP) and Yordan Alvarez (4 HR, 7 RBI) are as good a 1-2 punch as any in the league; the -0.61 luck delta is a real concern but the talent is undeniable | up

8. North Correa | Nico Hoerner (11 RBI) and a 5 QS week show real depth, but a 5.56 ERA and only 2 category wins in the matchup confirm this team needs pitching help badly despite the Ohtani and stolen-base upside | same

9. Hiroshima Radiation | Skubal and Eovaldi both posted sub-1.50 ERAs and the team ERA was a stellar 2.81, yet they lost 6-4 — the -1 NSVH is a killer and the offense's 6 HR and 1 SB simply aren't enough firepower | down

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A 4-4 tie feels like a missed opportunity against a weak Wombats squad, but Misiorowski (14 K) and a solid bullpen show real pitching upside; the offense needs more punch than Xavier Edwards walks can provide | same

11. Canberra Wombats | Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (5 R) gave the offense life in a tie, and 4 category wins is an improvement — but a .283 OBP and 49.2 IP are troublingly low roster-utilization numbers | up

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Junior Caminero (3 HR) and Bryan Reynolds (.500 OBP, 7 RBI) are legitimate weapons, but MacKenzie Gore's 6.52 ERA and Gausman's 1.55 WHIP continue to doom this team in the pitching categories | same

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Losing 7-2 to the hottest team in the league stings less than the underlying numbers — 33 hits, a .257 OBP, and only 2 category wins confirm this offense is broken, and dropping McGreevy without a real replacement is worrying | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 0.82 ERA was the lone bright spot in a 2-8 shellacking; with 4 HR, 45 K, and a +1.21 luck delta masking how bad things really are, this team is one average week away from a genuine crisis | down
